before_code:

after_code:
-- curated to minimal generality + real mathlib name + the actual criterion:
private theorem legendreSym_eq_one_of_ncard_primesOver_eq_finrank {ι : Type*} (d : ι → ℤ)
    (r : ι → K) (hr : ∀ i, r i ^ 2 = algebraMap ℤ K (d i)) [IsGalois ℚ K]
    {p : ℕ} [Fact p.Prime] {i : ι} (hcop_i : ¬ (p : ℤ) ∣ d i)
    (Q : Ideal (𝓞 K)) [Q.IsPrime] [Q.LiesOver (span {(p : ℤ)})]
    (hsplit : (primesOver (span {(p:ℤ)}) (𝓞 K)).ncard = finrank ℚ K) :
    legendreSym p (d i) = 1 := ...
-- [Finite ι] dropped (unused); hcop : ∀ i → pointwise hcop_i; name uses legendreSym + the real split criterion
description: Decomposition hygiene: when extracting helper lemmas from a monolithic proof, run /cleanup BEFORE opening the PR and curate each extracted lemma to the minimum its own statement needs. Mechanically-extracted helpers inherit the ambient context and get flagged by review. (1) GENERALITY: drop the enclosing section's unused typeclasses (e.g. [Finite ι]) and turn whole-family ∀-hypotheses (hcop : ∀ i, …) into pointwise ones (hcop_i) when the proof only uses the per-i fact. (2) NAMING: use the real mathlib object name (legendreSym, not legendre) and encode the actual hypothesis/criterion, not a vague label. (3) PLACEMENT: move generic infrastructure lemmas to the appropriate generic file as public lemmas instead of leaving them local to the specialized file. (4) DOCUMENTATION: keep helper docstrings statement-level; move proof narration into comments inside the proof. (5) PROOF-QUALITY: document inherited brittle steps (a fun _ => rfl defeq across a wrapper, show … by ring reshapes) with a one-line comment. The TauCeti review rubrics generality/placement/naming/documentation/proof-quality catch exactly these, so a pre-PR /cleanup pass avoids a round-trip.
